articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?

articles.headline: New Adidas World Cup jerseys rated: Argentina, Mexico, Spain
articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

article.headline: Brazil continue winning streak after late victory over Peru
article.description: Brazil made it two wins from two in 2026 World Cup qualifying on Tuesday after a late goal from Marquinhos earned them a 1-0 win over Peru in Lima.
article.linkText: Marquinhos earns Brazil late WCQ win over Peru

images.name: Neymar: I'm offside, we can move on
images.caption: Neymar and Richarlison speak on how VAR took seven minutes to check the Tottenham's star's goal before it was ruled out during Brazil's 1-0 win over Peru.

video.headline: Neymar: I'm offside, we can move on

article.story:
Brazil made it two wins from two in 2026 World Cup qualifying on Tuesday after a late goal from Marquinhos earned them a 1-0 win over Peru in Lima.

Brazil, who crushed Bolívia 5-1 at home on Friday, moved level on six points with rivals Argentina at the top of the South American qualifying standings.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Interim coach Fernando Diniz's side dominated possession and attacking opportunities early in the game but Raphinha and Richarlison saw goals ruled out for offside in the first half.

"I'm pleased with what we've shown in both games. Today's game was a different atmosphere. The team had to score three goals to make one count," Diniz told reporters.

"It's always important to win. I think we won with a lot of credit. I really enjoyed it."

With the game heading towards a scoreless draw Paris St Germain defender Marquinhos headed home Neymar's corner at the near post to seal the win in the 90th minute.

"We practised set pieces both in Belem and here. Set pieces define the game, today it did," Diniz added.

"We scored through persistence. A well struck ball and anticipation on the first post."

The five-times world champions will host Venezuela before visiting Uruguay next month when qualifying continues.

In other qualifiers earlier on Tuesday, Argentina cruised past Bolivia 3-0, Venezuela beat 1-0 Paraguay, Uruguay lost 2-1 at Ecuador, while Chile and Colombia drew 0-0.

videos.headline: Neymar: I'm offside, we can move on
videos.description: Neymar and Richarlison speak on how VAR took seven minutes to check the Tottenham's star's goal before it was ruled out during Brazil's 1-0 win over Peru.
